sanskrit:
सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद्ब्रह्मणो विदुः।
रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः॥
transliteration: sahasra-yuga-paryantam ahar yad brahmaṇo viduḥ / rātriṃ yuga-sahasrāntāṃ te ’ho-rātra-vido janāḥ
literal_meaning: Those who know the day of Brahmā as lasting a thousand yugas, and his night as ending after a thousand yugas, are the knowers of day and night.
contextual_meaning: Time is a psychological construct; cosmic scale relativizes personal urgency and anxiety.
psychological_state: Cognitive expansion that shrinks ego-based stress
trigger_scenario: Staring at starry sky after missing a deadline
anchor_text: Zoom out: cosmic time dissolves urgency into calm perspective.
